skills.name: doc-maintenance
skills.description: Keep project docs aligned with recent code and feature changes — detect drift, update affected pages, and add release-relevant notes without rewriting unchanged sections.

skills.name: issue-triage
skills.description: Triage Paperclip inbox issues that are stale, blocked, in-review, or assigned-but-not-progressing, and decide a single next action per issue (resume, reassign, unblock, escalate, or close).

skills.name: task-planning
skills.description: Turn a Paperclip issue or request into a structured implementation plan with child task graph, blockers, owners, and acceptance criteria, then save it as the issue `plan` document.

skills.name: wireframe
skills.description: Produce low-fidelity black-and-white UI wireframes as standalone SVG files, optionally bundled into a single-page HTML viewer and published via the here-now skill. Use when the user asks to "wireframe X", "sketch a screen for", "draft a layout", "low-fi mockup", "rough mock", "make a page to view the wireframes", "build a viewer for these screens", or to "deploy / publish / host the wireframes". Do NOT use when the user wants production UI code, branded designs, hi-fi mockups, or animated/interactive prototypes — use frontend-design or similar instead.

skills.name: qa-acceptance
skills.description: Produce QA acceptance criteria and a manual validation plan for a feature change — golden path, edge cases, error states, performance limits, and explicit pass/fail evidence.

skills.name: github-pr-workflow
skills.description: Prepare a GitHub pull request from a feature branch — branch hygiene, commit shape, title/body, verification notes, screenshots for UI work, and replies to review comments.

skills.name: agent-browser
skills.description: Drive a real browser to inspect or interact with a web page or app — navigate, take screenshots, read console and network, fill simple forms — for verification tasks, not unattended automation.

skills.name: release-announcement
skills.description: Write a release announcement — changelog, blog post, in-app note, or social post — that leads with user impact, names the audience, and includes upgrade/migration steps without filler.

skills.name: design-critique
skills.description: Give a structured product design critique — user job clarity, hierarchy, affordance, error states, accessibility, and consistency — focused on what to change, in what order, and why.

skills.name: last30days
skills.description: Research what people actually say about any topic in the last 30 days. Pulls posts and engagement from Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, Hacker News, Polymarket, GitHub, and the web.

skills.name: accessibility
skills.description: Gate de acessibilidade antes de qualquer UI ir para produção
